info.name: PAS — Keyword Search API
info.description: Endpoints over the keyword_searches store. (1) Store a search (frontend, JWT). (2) The single scraper 'work' endpoint — hit it in a loop with x-scraper-name; the server auto-closes the previous term (by name) and gives the next. (3) Insert SYNTHETIC keywords in bulk (CSV or JSON, network mandatory) — same collection + doc shape, marked users=null + userInfos=null. The /work claim also supports a synthetic-only mode (users:null) and, for the GOOGLE network, serves priority → user-searched → synthetic on every hit. A config-driven HARD cap (config.keywordSearch.cleanup, default 100k each for user-searched & synthetic) auto-deletes oldest scraped-first on insert. Set baseUrl, token, scraperName before running.

request.description: type: 1=keyword, 2=advertiser, 3=domain. network: a slug, comma list, or 'all'. Requires JWT in {{token}}. Re-run → status 'existing' (no duplicate).

First hit: completed=0 (nothing to close yet), returns one keyword in data[]. Each data[] item includes users: [{ id, username, email }] — everyone who searched that term, so you know whose request it is.

item.name: 4. Work: facebook (hit 2+ — auto-close prev, get next)

request.description: Same body as request 3. The server auto-closes the previous term by x-scraper-name (completed=1) and hands the next. Keep re-sending to loop. To mark the previous as no-ads, add: "status": "no_ads_found".
Even if facebook already scraped 'Nike' today, instagram still gets it (state is per-network).
Same auto-close behaviour, priority mode: only hands out freshly re-searched terms. Empty unless you store again first.

item.name: 7. Work: multi network + type (facebook OR instagram, keyword OR advertiser)

request.description: type and network may be arrays (or comma lists). The pool is the union of every type x network pair; each returned value carries its own concrete type+network, so a single value may come from facebook OR instagram and be a keyword OR an advertiser. Add "priority": true for the same behaviour in priority mode.

Bulk-insert manually-inserted (synthetic) keywords. NO JWT (internal). `network` is MANDATORY (batch field here, or per-item, or a CSV column) — it fills the doc's networks/networkState. Deduped case-insensitively by (type,valueNorm) via $setOnInsert (never clobbers an existing doc) — 'running shoes' and 'Running Shoes' collapse to one. Stored in the SAME collection + shape as user searches but with users=null + userInfos=null. JSON forms: ["a","b"], {"keywords":[...]}, or [{"value":"a","network":"facebook"}]. Response: { received, unique, inserted, duplicatesIgnored, skippedNoNetwork, cleanup }. Missing/invalid network → item skipped (skippedNoNetwork); none valid → 400.

Same endpoint via CSV upload (field name MUST be 'file', ≤50 MB, streamed). CSV = one keyword per line, OR a header row with a 'keyword' (or 'value') column (+ optional 'type'/'network' columns). The batch 'network'/'type' text fields here are the defaults when a row omits them. network is mandatory (batch field or per-row column). Pick a .csv in the 'file' row before sending.

Opt-in synthetic-only claim: send users:null (or userInfos:null) to claim ONLY synthetic (not-yet-user-searched) keywords — those with users/userInfos null. Omit it for the normal pool (unchanged). Response echoes synthetic:true.

item.name: 11. Work: google daily (priority → user → synthetic ordering)

request.description: GOOGLE-only: a normal daily claim (NO priority flag) serves terms in order on every hit — priority (networkState.google.isActive:true) first, then user-searched, then synthetic. Status transitions are unchanged (priority flips isActive; daily sets dailyClaimDate). Every other network and explicit priority:true are unchanged.
